title: Mean product lifetime of plastic uses
subtitle: Mean product lifetime (from production to disposal) of different uses of plastic products, measured in years.
caption:
Mean product lifetime of plastic uses

Mean product lifetime (from production to disposal) of different uses of plastic products, measured in years.

This horizontal bar chart displays the mean product lifetime of plastic uses in 2015, measured in years from production to disposal. The chart lists seven distinct categories of plastic application, arranged in descending order of longevity. Building and Construction represents the longest duration, with a mean lifetime of 35 years, extending significantly further to the right than any other category. Industrial Machinery follows with a value of 20 years, while Transportation sits at 13 years. The middle of the chart features Electrical/Electronic products at 8 years and Textiles at 5 years. The shortest lifetimes appear at the bottom of the list: Consumer & Institutional Products have a mean lifetime of 3 years, and Packaging has the briefest duration at 0.5 years. The visual gap between the top category and the bottom category is substantial, with the bar for Building and Construction being seventy times longer than the bar for Packaging. All bars are rendered in a uniform blue color against a white background, with the specific numerical value labeled clearly at the end of each bar. The data illustrates a wide range of usage periods, spanning from less than one year for packaging materials to several decades for construction applications.
